text.title: Gcp Networking Vpc Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gcp-networking-vpc methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Production VPCs MUST be custom-mode with explicit per-region subnets; auto-mode VPCs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Auto-mode allocates fixed-range subnets in every region — wastes IP space and conflicts with hybrid topologies.

rule.statement: Firewall rules MUST target service accounts (`--target-service-accounts`) rather than network tags wherever both are available.
rule.rationale: Service-account-based rules are identity-bound and survive instance replacement; tag-based rules drift.

rule.statement: Org- and folder-wide rules MUST be expressed via hierarchical firewall policies, not duplicated per-VPC.
rule.rationale: Policy duplication across VPCs is unauditable; hierarchical policies are inherited and centrally managed.

rule.statement: Production VPCs MUST have VPC Flow Logs enabled; security-sensitive subnets SHOULD additionally enable Packet Mirroring to an IDS.

rationale.source: VPC Flow Logs + Packet Mirroring docs

rule.rationale: Flow logs cover the metadata; Packet Mirroring covers the payload for high-sensitivity workloads.

rule.statement: Subnets hosting GKE clusters MUST declare secondary ranges for pods + services at creation time; retrofitting is impossible.
rule.rationale: GKE VPC-native (IP-aliasing) requires secondary ranges; adding them later requires cluster recreation.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
